newsBriefing: It was a landmark day across the global sports landscape, defined by historic firsts and the crowning of champions. On the collegiate stage, Duke secured its second consecutive ACC tournament title in a 74-70 win over Virginia, marking an unprecedented conference trifecta for the school after winning titles in football and women's basketball in the same year. In the WAC, California Baptist earned its first-ever NCAA tournament berth following a dramatic 63-61 victory sealed by a missed Utah Valley alley-oop in the closing seconds. International competition saw Venezuela make history by stunning defending champion Japan 8-5 to reach their first World Baseball Classic semifinal and qualify for the 2028 Olympics, while Formula 1 celebrated 19-year-old Kimi Antonelli becoming the second-youngest race winner ever at the Chinese Grand Prix. The day was further highlighted by Mikaela Shiffrin tying a record with her eighth World Cup slalom win of the season, Bryson DeChambeau surviving a wild Singapore playoff to claim his fourth LIV Golf title, and Luka Doncic delivering a signature game-winning shot for the Lakers in an overtime thriller against the Nuggets. Even as global tensions forced the cancellation of the highly anticipated Finalissima between Spain and Argentina, the day remained a showcase of veteran dominance and the arrival of a new generation of stars.
